findings.title: V4 legs decode the Universal Router payload twice before execution
findings.suggested_fix: Decode the V4 payload once per leg and perform the hook-data allowlist check inside the same UniversalRouterAdapter execution path, or return the decoded payload from a validation helper and pass it into executeV4. Preserve the raw execute rejection and hook-data key derivation, but avoid the current v4HookDataKey(data) abi.decode plus executeV4(data) abi.decode sequence for every V4 leg.

findings.title: Route-local accounting repeats snapshot scans and balance reads on the execution hot path
findings.suggested_fix: Precompute compact asset indexes while building snapshots and carry each leg's tokenIn/tokenOut indexes into execution, so baseline lookup is O(1). In the refund loop, compute current balance minus snapshots[i].baseline directly instead of calling _baseline again. Keep the external balance-delta invariant, but measure generated split routes and a max-leg synthetic route before accepting the optimization.

residual_risks: The TypeScript compiler and artifact writer currently operate over small static route and pool sets, so no generator-side path met the material-performance threshold. If this becomes graph search over a larger catalog, revisit pool lookup indexing and matrix generation.
No gas snapshot was run as part of this read-only review, so severity is based on code-path inspection rather than measured deltas.
testing_gaps: No Foundry gas benchmark covers generated V4-heavy routes before and after consolidating V4 payload decoding.
No gas benchmark covers generated split routes or a high-leg synthetic route for route-local accounting overhead.
